g.title: Complete Kit Disassembly: Exploded View
g.desc: This diagram is an exploded view, showing all the parts of your kit completely separated as if they've been taken apart. You'll find many pieces made of smooth, 5mm thick laser-cut plywood. Identify two long, curved 'arm' pieces, each with a large circular hole at one end, a smaller hole near the other, and a rectangular slot in the middle. There's also a small, flat plywood piece shaped like a wrench. The main structure includes several rectangular plywood plates of various sizes, featuring different shaped cutouts and round holes for fasteners. Scattered among these plywood parts are many tiny brass screws and nuts, shown separate from the pieces, indicating they have been unfastened. You'll also find a thin, smooth, light blue oval-rectangular plastic piece with two holes. Look for two thin, smooth, black rectangular strips, likely plastic or circuit boards; one has a small white connector on its end. At the bottom, there's a distinct orange, curved, trough-shaped plastic piece. Finally, locate a flexible electrical cable: one part has red and black wires ending in two pin connectors, and another part is a flat multicolored cable (white, black, red, yellow) ending in a white plastic plug. All pieces are shown distinct and separated, illustrating the kit in its fully disassembled state.

g.title: Kit Inventory List
g.desc: This instruction displays the kit's complete inventory list, showing the name and quantity of each part. It is intended for verifying that all components are present before starting any assembly or disassembly. The listed items fall into categories such as 5mm thick laser-cut plywood pieces (e.g., Large Base, Side Pieces, Ramps, Slopes, Arm Pieces), various plastic components (e.g., Protractors, Track, Spacers, Connectors), small electronic IR Beam Sensors, #4-40 brass hardware (including 1/2-inch and 1/4-inch pan-head screws, flat-head screws, and hex nuts), and rubber Gummy Feet. To identify and orient tactilely, locate and count each physical part in your kit, feeling their distinct shapes, textures, and materials: rigid 5mm plywood, smoother plastic, small metallic brass hardware, and soft flexible rubber. This specific step focuses solely on checking the contents against the provided list and does not involve any assembly or disassembly instructions.

g.title: Disconnect Wires and Remove Track
g.desc: This step shows how to disconnect wiring and remove the orange plastic track. First, locate the wiring at the end of the orange track. Feel two thin wires, one red and one brown, coming from the track and ending in two small metal pins. These pins plug into a small, rectangular white plastic connector block, which has a bundle of other multi-colored wires (like red, yellow, black, and white) coming out of its opposite side. Gently pull the two halves of this plastic connector apart to disconnect the wires. Next, identify the smooth, wide orange plastic track. It sits between two black plywood side rails, which are 5mm thick. Gently slide the orange plastic track forward and away from the rest of the kit assembly until it completely detaches from the black side rails.

g.title: Remove Slope and Protractor Pieces
g.desc: This step shows how to detach four specific plywood pieces from the main angled slope assembly. First, locate the two identical 'wrench-shaped' plywood pieces. They are flat, 5mm thick, and positioned one on each side of the upper part of the main slope structure. Each has a round hole at one end and an elongated slot at the other. Find the #4-40 brass screws and nuts holding them, unscrew them, and gently pull these 'wrench' pieces away from the slope. Next, find the longer, flat plywood piece attached to the lower section of the slope. This piece has one end that is rounded like a protractor, a long slot in its middle, and a small tab at the other end. Unscrew its #4-40 brass hardware and carefully detach it. Finally, locate the small rectangular plywood piece on the side of the bottom frame of the slope. This piece might have a small, raised electronic part at one end. Remove the #4-40 brass screws holding it and carefully pull it free from the frame.

g.title: Remove Arms and Ramp Piece
g.desc: This step shows how to disassemble the arm and ramp components. First, locate the two long, curved 'arm' pieces made of 5mm thick plywood, one on each side of the central structure. Each arm is held in place by two small #4-40 brass screws. Feel for the round heads of these screws, one at the top and one at the bottom of each arm where it connects to the main assembly. Use a screwdriver to unscrew all four screws. As you unscrew them, reach around to the back to feel for the small hexagonal brass nuts and remove those as well. Once the screws and nuts are removed, gently pull each arm piece outwards and away from the main structure. Finally, with the arms removed, you will find the 'ramp' piece – a flatter, rectangular plywood piece with several square slots – sitting loosely in the middle. Carefully lift this ramp piece straight up to remove it.

g.title: Remove Side Panels and Components
g.desc: You are now disassembling the main unit. First, locate the two long, flat, 5mm thick plywood side pieces attached to the longer edges of the central main assembly. One side piece is a natural wood color, and the other is black. Feel for four brass #4-40 screws and nuts that secure these side pieces to the main assembly; there are two screws per side piece. Use your screwdriver to unscrew and remove these four screws and their corresponding nuts. Gently detach both the natural wood and the black side pieces from the main assembly and set them aside. Next, on the central main assembly, locate the oval-shaped, light blue component attached to its underside. It is held by two brass #4-40 screws and nuts. Unscrew these two screws and remove the nuts, then separate the light blue component from the main assembly. Finally, pick up the black side piece you just removed. Feel for a small, black, rectangular plastic component embedded in it, which has a textured face. It is held by two brass #4-40 screws. Unscrew these two screws to separate the black plastic component from the black side piece.

g.title: Detach Sensor from Plywood Bracket
g.desc: Locate a flat, black rectangular sensor piece connected to a 5mm thick plywood bracket. The plywood bracket has rounded corners and several cutout shapes, including two large horizontal slots and smaller circular holes. The black sensor piece has a small rectangular sensor module at one end. Two #4-40 brass screws hold the sensor to the plywood bracket. Feel for these two screws. To disassemble, unscrew and remove both brass screws, then gently pull the black sensor piece away from the plywood bracket.

g.title: Disassemble Track Connector, Spacer, Base
g.desc:
This instruction guides you to take apart three connected plywood pieces: the Track Connector, a Track Spacer, and the Small Base. First, locate the assembly, which is a stack of three flat, 5mm thick laser-cut plywood pieces held together by two #4-40 brass screws.
To identify the pieces tactually: * The **top piece** is the Track Connector. It feels like a rectangular piece with gently rounded corners and two smooth holes, one near each end. * The **middle piece** is the Track Spacer. It is also rectangular but noticeably shorter than the Track Connector, with two holes that align with the others. * The **bottom piece** is the Small Base. It's the largest of the three, feeling like a wider rectangle with two square-shaped cutouts, one in the middle of each long side, and two holes that align with the others.
To disassemble, use a screwdriver to carefully unscrew and remove the two #4-40 brass screws. Once the screws are out, gently lift and separate all three pieces from each other.

g.title: DISASSEMBLY COMPLETE!
g.desc: If not already done, place each piece on its spot on the components sheet.

text.tspan: If not already done, place each piece on its spot on the components sheet
